description: Port of nanotags - a tiny, typed Custom Elements wrapper with reactive props, automatic cleanup, no Shadow DOM, and hydration-first design - swapping the reactivity engine from nanostores to alien-signals. Reactive props are exposed as native callable signals (count() reads, count(v) writes). Ships three entrypoints: microtags, microtags/render, microtags/context.

userStories.title: Project setup and package identity
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the repo configured as the microtags package with the right dependencies and export map so the ported modules resolve correctly.
acceptanceCriteria: package.json name set to the microtags package name; template placeholders ({{package-name}}, {{repo-name}}, {{component-name}}) replaced or removed
alien-signals added as a dependency
@standard-schema/spec added as a dependency
exports map exposes '.', './render', and './context' subpaths in both ESM and CJS, matching the existing dual-format build
sideEffects: false is set in package.json
npm run build produces dist/index, dist/render, dist/context in both ESM and CJS with type declarations
Typecheck passes
Lint passes

userStories.title: Port shared types and utils
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the foundational types.ts and utils.ts ported so the rest of the modules have their primitives.
acceptanceCriteria: src/types.ts ports nanotags' shared types, adjusted so reactive values are typed as alien-signals signals rather than nanostores atoms
src/types.ts declares the SetupContext (ctx) type shape so factory and define can typecheck against it before setup-context is implemented
src/utils.ts ports nanotags' shared helpers
No reference to nanostores remains in either file
Typecheck passes
Lint passes

userStories.title: Reactivity adapter over alien-signals
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a small internal reactivity layer (signal, effect, effectScope, peek, untracked) so every nanostores call site maps cleanly onto alien-signals.
acceptanceCriteria: Internal helpers created for: creating a signal, reading a signal, writing a signal, running an auto-tracked effect, and disposing a group of effects via effectScope
A prop value handed to user code is the native alien-signals callable signal (count() to read, count(v) to write)
untracked(fn) reads inside fn without creating a subscription, implemented via setCurrentSub(undefined) with restore in a finally
peek(signal) returns the signal's current value without subscribing (peek(s) === untracked(() => s()))
peek and untracked are exported from the main entrypoint (src/index.ts)
Effects created during a component's setup() are grouped in an effectScope so they can be disposed together on disconnect
Typecheck passes
Lint passes

userStories.title: Port the custom-element factory and lifecycle
userStories.description: As a developer, I need factory.ts ported so define() produces a real custom element with the nanotags lifecycle and cleanup scope.
acceptanceCriteria: Factory produces an HTMLElement subclass with no Shadow DOM
connectedCallback hydrates props from attributes and runs setup()
attributeChangedCallback updates the corresponding prop signal
disconnectedCallback runs all registered cleanups
Reconnecting re-runs setup() with a fresh cleanup scope while preserving programmatically-set prop values
If a cleanup throws, the remaining cleanups still run and the first error re-throws afterward
Typecheck passes
Lint passes

userStories.title: Port the withProps builder (coercers and Standard Schema)
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the withProps part of builders.ts ported so props are declared with typed, inferred coercers and validators.
acceptanceCriteria: withProps((p) => ...) supports the built-in coercers (number, string, boolean, json)
withProps accepts any Standard Schema validator (e.g. Zod, Valibot, ArkType) for a prop
Attribute-backed, JSON, and property-only prop variants supported
Props are type-inferred through the chain into setup()
Typecheck passes
Lint passes

userStories.title: Port the withRefs and withContexts builders
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the withRefs and withContexts parts of builders.ts ported so components declare typed, inferred refs and context dependencies.
acceptanceCriteria: withRefs((r) => ...) resolves single (r.one) and multiple (r.all) refs via [data-ref="name"] selectors
withContexts((c) => ...) declares context dependencies
Refs and contexts are type-inferred through the chain into setup()
Typecheck passes
Lint passes

userStories.title: Port the setup context (ctx)
userStories.description: As a developer, I need setup-context.ts ported so the ctx object passed to setup() exposes the nanotags API backed by alien-signals.
acceptanceCriteria: ctx.host is the element; ctx.props are callable signals; ctx.refs are resolved refs; ctx.contexts are resolved contexts
ctx.peek(signal) reads a signal's value without subscribing, even when called inside a ctx.effect
ctx.on(target, type, handler) adds a listener, auto-removed on disconnect
ctx.effect(...) runs an alien-signals effect that auto-tracks the signals it reads and is auto-disposed on disconnect
ctx.bind(target, property, signal) two-way binds a DOM property to a signal, auto-cleaned on disconnect
ctx.onCleanup(fn) registers custom teardown run on disconnect
ctx.consume(token) reads an optional context
Typecheck passes
Lint passes

userStories.title: Port the define() fluent entry
userStories.description: As a developer, I need define.ts ported so the chainable builder ties the pieces together and registers the element.
acceptanceCriteria: define(tagName) returns a builder with .withProps, .withRefs, .withContexts, and .setup
Calling .setup(callback) registers the custom element
The chain compiles with full inference (props/refs/contexts visible and correctly typed inside setup())
Exported from src/index.ts
Typecheck passes
Lint passes

userStories.title: Port cross-component context (microtags/context)
userStories.description: As a developer, I need context.ts ported so components can provide and consume shared context, deferring setup until required contexts resolve.
acceptanceCriteria: microtags/context entrypoint exports the context creation / provide / consume API matching nanotags
A component declaring required contexts via .withContexts() does not run setup() until those contexts resolve
Context values are backed by alien-signals so consumers react to changes
./context subpath resolves in both ESM and CJS with type declarations
Typecheck passes
Lint passes

userStories.title: Port the render helpers (microtags/render)
userStories.description: As a developer, I need render.ts ported so the templating helpers are available from the ./render subpath.
acceptanceCriteria: microtags/render entrypoint exports the render/templating helpers with the same signatures as nanotags
Helpers are tree-shakeable (isolated subpath, sideEffects: false)
./render subpath resolves in both ESM and CJS with type declarations
Typecheck passes
Lint passes

userStories.title: Test harness plus define and reactive-props tests

acceptanceCriteria: npm test runs the browser tests via esbuild then tapout
Tests cover define registering a working custom element
Tests cover reactive props: reading, writing, and reacting (an effect re-runs when a prop signal changes)

Tests pass via npm test

acceptanceCriteria: Tests cover hydrating props from attributes on connect
Tests cover attributeChangedCallback updating the matching prop signal
Tests cover refs: r.one resolves a single [data-ref], r.all resolves multiple
No assertions on specific rendered text or HTML strings

userStories.title: Context provide/consume and deferred-setup tests

acceptanceCriteria: Tests cover providing and consuming a context across components
Tests cover deferred setup: setup() does not run until required contexts resolve
Tests cover consumers reacting to context value changes
No assertions on specific rendered text or HTML strings

userStories.title: Untracked-read and cleanup tests
userStories.description: As a developer, I need untracked reads and the automatic-cleanup guarantee verified.
acceptanceCriteria: Test: a peek/untracked read inside an effect does NOT re-run the effect when that signal later changes
Tests cover cleanup of listeners (ctx.on), effects (ctx.effect), bindings (ctx.bind), and onCleanup callbacks on disconnect
Test: throwing-cleanup behavior - remaining cleanups still run and the first error re-throws afterward
No assertions on specific rendered text or HTML strings
Tests pass via npm test
Typecheck passes
Lint passes

userStories.title: Update README, example, and docs
userStories.description: As a user, I want documentation that reflects the microtags API and the alien-signals reactivity model.
acceptanceCriteria: README documents define, props, refs, contexts, the ctx API (including peek / untracked for non-subscribing reads), and both sub-entrypoints, using the callable-signal read/write form
README notes the one divergence from nanotags: native alien-signals (callable) replace nanostores atoms; $-prefixed atom names become plain callable signal names
The example/ app uses the ported API and runs
Typecheck passes
Lint passes
Verify the example in the browser using the dev-browser skill
